design_name: 180-degree U-bend (return bend) pipe fitting swept in the XZ plane, with two parallel vertical legs 70mm apart and a semicircular crown of 35mm centerline radius.
prompt: A 180-degree U-bend (return bend) pipe fitting with the bend in the XZ plane. The pipe has a 20mm outer diameter and 16mm inner diameter (2mm wall thickness). The bend centerline follows a semicircular arc of 35mm radius on the XZ plane: starting at the origin heading in the +Z direction, arcing upward through Z=35 at the apex, and returning down to (X=70, Z=0) heading in the -Z direction. The two pipe legs are parallel, 70mm apart in the X direction. The part is centered at Y=0. Created by sweeping the pipe cross-section along the arc path defined by a radiusArc from (0,0) to (70,0) with radius 35 on the XZ workplane.
primitive_plan_summary: 180-degree U-bend (return bend) pipe fitting swept in the XZ plane, with two parallel vertical legs 70mm apart and a semicircular crown of 35mm centerline radius.

verifier_score.feedback: All constraints met. The U-bend pipe fitting is correctly constructed: (1) The bounding box X-span is 90mm (70mm leg separation + 2×10mm outer radius = 90mm, correct). (2) The Y-span is 20mm matching the 20mm outer diameter. (3) The Z-span is 45mm (35mm bend radius + 10mm outer radius = 45mm, correct). (4) The part is centered at Y=0 (ymin=-10, ymax=+10). (5) The center of mass at X=35 confirms symmetry about the midpoint between the two legs. (6) The volume of ~12,436 mm³ is plausible for a hollow semicircular pipe (theoretical: π × 35 × π × (10²-8²) ≈ 12,425 mm³, matching within 0.08%). (7) The rendered images show a proper U-bend shape with semicircular arc in the XZ plane, two parallel legs, and hollow pipe ends visible. (8) 4 faces (outer surface, inner surface, two annular end faces) and 6 edges are consistent with a hollow swept semicircular tube. The part is valid, watertight, and has no self-intersections.

(5) The center of mass at X=35 confirms symmetry about the midpoint between the two legs. (6) The volume of ~12,436 mm³ is plausible for a hollow semicircular pipe (theoretical: π × 35 × π × (10²-8²) ≈ 12,425 mm³, matching within 0.08%). (7) The rendered images show a proper U-bend shape with semicircular arc in the XZ plane, two parallel legs, and hollow pipe ends visible. (8) 4 faces (outer surface, inner surface, two annular end faces) and 6 edges are consistent with a hollow swept semicircular tube. The part is valid, watertight, and has no self-intersections.
